title: Move release image builds off the e2-micro VM
goal: Stop building production Docker images on the low-cost e2-micro VM and make deployments run prebuilt images instead.
context: During the first manual GCP release, docker compose up --build on the e2-micro VM stalled the machine while building the web, API, and MCP images.
The web npm ci step and Go builds consumed enough CPU and memory that SSH became unreliable even after adding swap.
The e2-micro VM is acceptable as a low-traffic runtime target, but it is not an acceptable image build target.
scope: Build web, API, and MCP Docker images in GitHub Actions or another external builder.
Push versioned images to a registry such as GitHub Container Registry or Google Artifact Registry.
Update the production Compose deployment to reference image tags instead of local build contexts.
Keep the VM deployment path limited to docker login, docker compose pull or docker load, migrations, compose up, and smoke checks.
Preserve the current low-cost single-VM runtime model.
verification: A deployment to staging completes without running Docker build steps on the VM.
The VM remains reachable over SSH during staging and production deploys.
Smoke checks pass after running the stack from prebuilt images.
Rollback can redeploy a previous image tag without rebuilding on the VM.
notes: GitHub Actions now builds release images away from the VM.
Artifact Registry is used when the repository exists, with the previous image archive path retained as a fallback.
The VM pulls immutable commit-SHA image tags using the VM service account and retags them to the local Compose project tags.
Registry-backed deployment was verified through staging and production.
